items.title: How beer and AI are changing the course of Canberra's economic future

children.content: The capital has long been known as a public service town, but with about two in three workers now employed in the private sector and the population expected to reach nearly 700,000 by 2050, is that reputation — and key economic driver — likely to change?

items.title: 'Not what the community wanted': Big Splash lease spared despite backlash

children.content: ACT planning authorities have decided they will not follow through with a threat to terminate the lease of Big Splash water park, saying they are now satisfied the operators have made moves to reopen later this year.

image.altText: Three tower waterslides, all in a significant state of disrepair, surrounded by gum trees.

items.title: Want to buy a football team? Why this club's next chapter is unwritten

children.content: Canberra United is once again without an owner and facing an uncertain future, in a tale all too familiar for players and fans of the A-League's only stand-alone women's team.

image.altText: Soccer players wearing green, black and white uniforms smile at each other during a game

items.title: 'Carry the hope': Thousands gather for Anzac Day service in Canberra

children.content: Prime Minister Anthony Albanese and New Zealand High Commissioner Andrew Needs are among those to gather in Canberra for the 111th anniversary of the Gallipoli landings.

gallery.altText: The facade of the Australian War Memorial was lit up before dawn on Anzac Day 2026.

items.title: Canberrans with disability fear NDIS changes will cut vital support

children.content: Canberrans living with disability warn planned changes to the NDIS could strip back support, undermining their mental health and sense of certainty.

image.altText: A woman sitting on a couch looking at the camera.

items.title: Payroll error leaves thousands of hospital staff waiting for fortnightly wages

children.content: Thousands of North Canberra Hospital employees were not paid on time this fortnight, with some staff raising concerns on social media that they had received pay slips but no corresponding bank deposits, after a payroll processing issue.

image.altText: North Canberra Hospital exterior

items.title: 'Self-belief, respect': Indigenous boxers building strength with David Pocock

children.content: A Canberra Indigenous-led boxing camp is helping young fighters hone their skills for life inside and outside the ring in its first development camp, continuing a proud tradition of First Nations involvement in the sport.

gallery.altText: A young boy hits a punching bag in a boxing gym.

thumbnail.altText: A young boy hits a punching bag in a boxing gym.

items.title: An oil shock, a federal budget and the recession we may have to have

children.content: Accompanying the oil shock unfolding in the Strait of Hormuz is a profound change in the way fiscal and monetary policymakers operate. And as the government prepares the budget, with the impact of the Iran war still unfolding, a recession may be ahead.

image.altText: A man in a suit from a side profile with his chin jutted out defiantly.

items.title: Trump turmoil is political kryptonite in Australia

children.content: As Donald Trump's approval in Australia sinks, Anthony Albanese needs to walk a careful line: keep voters convinced of the merits of the alliance, while avoiding the impression he is being too reverential.

image.altText: Anthony Albanese takes a selfie with a smiling Donald Trump

items.title: A fascinating admission reveals truth beneath the chest beating in this war

children.content: It's a ceasefire in name only between Israel and its targets in Lebanon, as the wait continues for more talks between the US and Iran.

articles.title: Iranian foreign minister heads to Russia for meeting with Vladimir Putin

children.content: Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi will meet Russian President Vladimir Putin during a visit to Moscow later today, the country's ISNA news agency reports. Follow live.

items.title: The Istanbul hideout: Secret files expose Australia's underworld empire offshore

children.content: Istanbul was once the hideout of choice for Australian crooks. Now a Sydney gangster's legal challenge has revealed how it all unravelled for the "Aussie Cartel".

items.title: More families are sleeping in cars. But they're not the highest housing priority

children.content: An analysis of 12 months of urgent housing and homelessness attendances in Tasmania has shown an alarming spike in people with dependent children needing help — while overcrowding is an increasing concern.

children.content: Australian Bushmaster armoured vehicles are being sent to the Netherlands following a request to Australia by the Dutch government.

children.content: The S&P 500 rallied to yet another record close as first-quarter earnings continued to beat expectations, but the ASX is priced for a muted opening. Follow the day's events and insights from our business reporters on the ABC News live markets blog.

items.title: US allies turning to Japan for defence supplies as Iran War drags on

items.title: Two-hour barrier smashed twice in history-making London Marathon

children.content: Kenya's Sabastian Sawe has become the first man to run a marathon in under two hours, winning the London Marathon with a time of 1:59:30.

items.title: Foord stars for Arsenal, Kerr equals record in WSL

items.title: AI is helping rangers protect Victorian wildlife in a fraction of the time

items.title: Subdivisions taking place in Adelaide's 'visionary' Hills Face Zone

children.content: The Hills Face Zone was legislated to protect the natural character of Adelaide's landscape backdrop but, despite subdivisions not being permitted, there have been 11 over the past decade.

image.altText: Face of hillside stretching into distance with some houses among trees
